MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PCN-18-301651 Conservatorship of Connie M. Martas and PTR-19-303276 Connie M. Martas Trust.

Appearances: James Bridgeman appeared in person for Florence Seebach (petitioner).

The Court advised counsel to file a Petition for Termination.

Mr. Bridgeman requests more time to file his papers.

Both matters are continued to July 13 , 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

The Second Accounting is continued from May 26, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. To August 17, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Michael McNaughton. Clerk: A. Datt. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jul-16-2025 Continued To Aug-27-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
The following matters were heard: PCN-18-301651 Conservatorship of Connie Maratas and PTR-19-303276 Connie Mary Maratas 1997 Trust.

Appearances: James Bridgeman appeared in person for Florence Seebach (conservator).
Madeline Eddington appeared in person, (pro per) (former temporary conservator).

Ms. Eddington stated that in 2017 she became the temporary conservator of her aunt, that she was on her advanced healthcare directive, that she seeks reimbursement of $9000.00 she paid for her aunt's care, and that she has copies of her aunt's trust documents. Ms. Eddington also presented all documents to Mr. Bridgeman in open Court, and that she is willing to resend the documents to him.

The Court instructed Mr. Bridgeman to lodge both trust documents with the Court.

Mr. Bridgeman stated that he's been having trouble receiving documents from two bank institutions Citibank and Chase Bank, that he is looking into filing subpoenas for the records, that he had no issues paying Ms. Eddington the fees she is requesting.

Both matters are continued to August 27, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Apr-07-2025 Continued To Jul-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together:
PCN-18-301651, Conservatorship of Connie M. Maratas; and
PTR-19-303276, Connie Mary Maratas 1997 Trust

Appearances:
James Bridgman for Florence Seebach, Conservator and Successor Trustee.

Discussion was held regarding deficiencies in the accountings. Mr. Bridgman stated that an additional accounting covering April 1, 2021, through the date of death (January 2024) is still in progress. He asserted that all requirements related to the current accountings had been met, and requested that those be approved by the court.

The court continued the matter to July 16, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Mr. Bridgman was directed to file complete copies of both trusts in the trust matter. The Court suggested that a petition be filed to determine which trust instrument governs, with notice to all stakeholders.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-15-2023 Continued To Apr-12-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-18-301651 Conservatorship of Connie M. Maratas and
PTR-19-303276 The Connie Mary Maratas Trust.

Appearances: James Bridgman appeared by zoom for and with Florence Seebach (conservator) by zoom.
Mary Gemma O'Keeffe (court appointed counsel) appeared by zoom for conservatee.

Mrs. O'Keeffe stated that if she could, she waives the notice issue, that she spoke to one relative who is aware of the move, and that the care manager is also well aware. Mrs. O'Keeffe also stated that Mrs. Maratas is on hospice, but not actively dying, which is why they don't know how long she will be at the new location, and that the conservator feels it is an appropriate time to move her. Mrs. Seebach spoke of Mrs. Maratas' medical condition, stated that Mrs. Maratas is doing as well as can be expected, but they need to look at her finances, and that it is important to have her stay at a place where she can get the care she needs for a less amount. Mr. Bridgman stated that Mrs. Maratas has 24-hour care and has a Kaiser hospice team. Mrs. O'Keeffe stated that she is not objecting, that she understands there is a money issue, that the owner of the new place knows Connie and can help with regard to training of staff as to what Connie's needs are, that Sage feels the critical time is the actual movement from the home to the new place, and that the sage people will be there during the move and be part of the transition. Mrs. Seebach stated that she and her brothers will stay with Connie for a week, that the current caregiver will be there with Connie the first week, that the caregivers are well trained and that she does not think the sage person will need to be there. The court acknowledges concern about someone in Connie's state being moved, stated not to skimp and encouraged Mrs. Seebach to give sage their role. The First Account and Report of Conservator, etc., and the First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. are continued to April 12, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition for an Order Changing the Residence of Conservatee, etc. is under submission with the court's intent to grant.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-19-2022 Continued To Feb-15-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman, via Zoom, for and with Florence Seebach, Conservator/Trustee.

Mr. Bridgman and Ms. Seebach report as to Ms. Maratas' current health status and associated costs for care. Mr. Bridgman requests permission to move Ms. Maratas to a different, less costly facility. The court offers its position regarding the request and advises that it is not denying the request and that Ms. Maratas' care is its primary concern. Mr. Bridgman acknowledges the amendments required to the accounting in the Conservatorship and related trust matters and requests a continuance to complete and file them.

The matter is continued to Feb-15-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman, via ZOOM, for Florence Seebach, Trustee.

The court asks for an update regarding the Accounting in this matter. Mr. Bridgman reports of an issue caused by a Charles Schwab account which had been radically undervalued on a previously filed Inventory and Appraisal (I&A) and a corrected I&A has been filed accordingly. Mr. Bridgman reports that there have been difficulties with finalizing the accounting.

After additional discussion, the Future Accounting is continued to Apr-25-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Appearances: James Bridgman, via ZOOM, for Florence Seebach, via ZOOM, Petitioner/Conservator.

The court and Mr. Bridgman discuss bond amount in both the trust and related conservatorship matter. Mr. Bridgman is also instructed to confirm whether the bond amount in the conservatorship is adequate.

Petition For Order Appointing Florence Seebach As Successor Trustee, Bringing Trusts Under Continuing Court Supervision, And Setting Trustee'S Bond
Off Calendar Dec-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman appeared by zoom for Florence Seebach (Petitioner).
Mary Gemma O'Keeffe Court appointed Counsel for Conservatee appeared by zoom.

The Court stated that it thinks the bond amount is appropriate and questions why Ms. O'Keeffe is requesting language that the Conservatee not be relocated without with a court order when it is already covered in the conservatorship matter. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that she is just doing her due diligence, that she wanted to make it's not lost and that she is okay as long as it's noted that if relocating the Conservatee were to happen, it would require a petition in the conservatorship. Mr. Bridgman stated that he agrees. The Court will take said language out of the order. The Court will also attempt to line up the accountings in both the conservatorship and the trust. The Petition for Order Appointing Florence Seebach as Successor/Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.
